skill.name: ui-final-polish
skill.description: Apply a restrained final polish pass to an existing UI component or screen. This skill improves spacing, alignment, text hierarchy, readability, shadows, highlights, effects, and action placement while preserving the current design direction. It is not a redesign skill.
skill.summary: Restrained visual polish for existing UI components and screens without redesigning them.
skill.author: JohnWayneeee

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The 33 external_commands alerts were markdown inline code backticks (for example `ui-final-polish` and `blur = y * 2`), not shell execution. The 50 weak cryptographic algorithm alerts matched markdown bullet dashes and heading hashes. The 12 system reconnaissance alerts matched normal design documentation phrases. The single hardcoded URL is a benign product mention (casely.digital) at the end of the file. No malicious intent or dangerous patterns were found.

low_findings.title: Benign hardcoded URL
low_findings.description: The file contains a link to https://casely.digital/ as a product mention. This is not data exfiltration or a malicious endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is a passive product mention in documentation text, not an active network request or exfiltration pattern.

content.value_statement: Existing UI often feels unfinished even when the structure is correct. This skill applies surgical visual fixes to spacing, hierarchy, alignment, and effects so the interface looks production-ready while keeping the original design intact.
actual_capabilities: Tighten or loosen section gaps and padding rhythm
Improve text hierarchy by adjusting contrast, size, and weight
Fix alignment and composition issues in existing layouts
Tune shadows, highlights, and edge definitions for depth
Anchor actions and buttons more clearly within cards or forms
Verify polish results against the original design intent
limitations: Does not redesign or change the visual direction of a component
Does not create new design systems or broad token replacements
Requires the user to name the specific node or issue to fix
Does not add or remove product content during polish

use_cases.title: Polish a dashboard card before release
use_cases.description: A product team has a functional dashboard card with correct data, but the spacing feels cramped and the action button looks detached. The skill adjusts padding, anchors the button, and calms secondary text.

use_cases.title: Fix hierarchy in a settings form
use_cases.description: A settings screen has labels, values, and helper text that all compete for attention. The skill quiets helper copy, strengthens labels, and creates a clear reading order.

use_cases.title: Refine glass-like panels in a dark UI
use_cases.description: A dark-mode interface uses translucent panels that make text hard to read. The skill adjusts backdrop blur, panel tint, and inner highlights to restore legibility without losing the glass aesthetic.

prompt_templates.title: Basic polish request
prompt_templates.prompt: Polish this card component. Keep the current style. Fix spacing and make the primary action clearer.

prompt_templates.title: Targeted spacing fix
prompt_templates.prompt: The spacing between the header and content in this modal feels off. Do a tiny polish pass on that gap only. Preserve everything else.

prompt_templates.title: Hierarchy and contrast tune
prompt_templates.prompt: Improve the text hierarchy in this settings panel. Lower the contrast of helper text, strengthen labels, and keep the current color palette. Do not redesign.

prompt_templates.title: Glass panel refinement
prompt_templates.prompt: This glass-like floating dock in a dark UI is hard to read. Adjust the panel tint, backdrop blur, and top highlight so text is legible while keeping the glass feel. Compare before and after visually.

output_examples.input: Polish this card. The button feels detached and the title does not stand out.
output_examples.output: I preserved the card's existing color palette and corner radius. I tightened the gap between the content block and the action row from 24px to 16px so the button sits closer to its context. I increased the title font weight from 500 to 600 and reduced subtitle opacity from 80% to 60% so the hierarchy is clearer. The button now aligns with the card's horizontal padding system.

output_examples.input: Fix the glass panel in this dark UI. The text is hard to read.
output_examples.output: I kept the glass material language. I darkened the panel fill from 30% to 50% opacity and increased backdrop blur from 20 to 48. I added a subtle top-edge inner highlight at 1px with 50% white opacity to define the surface. The text now reads comfortably without losing the see-through character.

best_practices: Start with a tiny polish budget and expand only if the user asks for more
Preserve the current visual language, density, and dark or light mood
Fix spacing and alignment before adding new effects or decoration
anti_patterns: Redesigning the component while calling it polish
Changing multiple unrelated areas when the user named one issue
Adding heavy shadows and glows on every layer at once

faq.question: What is the difference between polish and redesign?
faq.answer: Polish makes the existing design cleaner and more production-ready without changing its direction. Redesign creates a new visual direction, replaces the aesthetic, or rethemes colors and typography.

faq.question: When should I use this skill instead of pencil-ui-structure?
faq.answer: Use pencil-ui-structure first when you need to structure, name, organize, package, or hand off a design for code. Use ui-final-polish afterward for visual refinement of an already structured component.

faq.question: Can this skill change the color palette?
faq.answer: No. The skill preserves the existing color palette unless the user explicitly asks for a redesign or design-system update.

faq.question: What does a tiny polish budget mean?
faq.answer: A tiny polish budget means making one to three property changes on the named node or its direct children. It is the default starting point to avoid over-editing.

faq.question: Does this skill work for code components?
faq.answer: Yes. For code components, the skill prefers adjusting hierarchy, spacing, and composition while preserving the existing design system and tokens.

faq.question: Will the skill add new visual effects automatically?
faq.answer: No. New effects are added only when the component already uses that material language, the problem is about depth or separation, and the user did not ask to keep the current material exactly.
